Patient: Honestly, I'm not really sure why I'm here. I mean, I know I should quit, but it's just so hard. I've tried before, and it didn't work out.
Therapist: I understand. Quitting can be tough, but it's great that you're considering it again. Can you tell me more about your previous attempts to quit? What worked and what didn't?

Patient: Well, the first time I tried to quit, I used nicotine gum, and it was okay for a few months. But then I started smoking again. The second time, I got a prescription medication, but it was really expensive, and I couldn't afford it for long. To be honest, that's one of my biggest concerns – the cost. I'm a single mom, and I barely make ends meet as it is.
